trade.summary: Solid bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and precision instruments. Israel exports diamonds (processed), pharmaceuticals, and high-tech products. A bilateral free trade agreement is in place.

diplomatic.summary: Switzerland maintains friendly relations with Israel while also engaging with Palestinian leadership through its humanitarian role. As a neutral state, Switzerland hosts international dialogues on Middle East issues. Geneva hosts various diplomatic efforts related to the region. The Gaza conflict since 2023 has created some diplomatic tension.

military.summary: No military alliance. Switzerland's neutrality precludes defense cooperation. Switzerland maintains strict arms export controls. Some Swiss companies have dual-use technology relationships with Israeli defense firms.

financial.summary: Switzerland manages significant Israeli private wealth. Swiss banks are active in Israeli financial markets. Israeli tech companies access European capital markets through Switzerland. Significant bilateral investment in pharmaceuticals and technology.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Some cooperation on solar energy technology. Israel's natural gas exports may eventually reach Switzerland through European networks.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific collaboration in biotechnology, computer science, and materials science. Swiss and Israeli universities maintain active exchange programs. Joint publications in life sciences and physics are substantial.
